title: A Systematic Approach to Experimenting with Gen AI
content:
To reduce risk, refine their strategies, and optimize adoption at scale, companies need a more systematic approach to experimenting with generative AI at the organizational level. After taking the software industry by storm, generative AI is now moving into a broad set of industries, including manufacturing. In these physical industries, AI is helping managers navigate unpredictability and support real-time decision-making on the factory floor. Generative AI’s ability to codify, automate, and distribute organizational expertise may eventually reshape work structures from the shop floor to the C-suite. Some companies are already using it to analyze the flood of information generated in factories, predict problems, and simulate complex scenarios.

By working with a wide range of manufacturing industry data—from maintenance manuals and machine automation code to 3D drawings—generative AI has the potential to establish new ways for people and machines to collaborate. However, determining who will benefit from these changes and how quickly they will occur remains a complex question. Like electricity and the printing press, generative AI is a general-purpose technology, meaning its adoption and integration will rarely be straightforward. Managers often fail to recognize the true economic potential of such new technologies and struggle to reorganize tasks, skills, and workflows to suit them.

As a result, organizational performance gains typically lag behind technological diffusion, giving rise to what is known as the "productivity J curve." This phenomenon describes an initial dip in productivity as organizations struggle to adapt to a new technology, followed by sustained gains once complementary investments finally pay off. Recent data on generative AI is highly consistent with that historical pattern of technological adoption. A 2025 McKinsey survey, for instance, found that although many firms had rapidly adopted generative AI, more than 80% of respondents reported that it had no significant impact on their earnings yet.
